tasks.title: Add Elon death check to battery explosion
tasks.description: Extend processSubparBatteryExplosions() in game.js to check if Elon is adjacent to an exploding battery and roll a 10% kill chance.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
1. When a Subpar Battery explodes, check all 8 adjacent tiles for Elon.
2. If Elon is adjacent, roll 10% chance to destroy him.
3. On success: set name to 'Elon Bones', movesLeft=0, movesMax=0, state.gameOver=true.
4. Add log entry with type 'explosion' describing Elon's death.
5. Battery still explodes and is removed regardless of Elon check.
6. Rocktimus Robots are NOT affected.
tasks.claudeMd:
# Battery Explosion Elon Death

You are modifying `game.js`, specifically the `processSubparBatteryExplosions()` function (around line 1739).

## What to do

After the existing explosion log + splice (line 1745-1746), add code to:
1. Find Elon: iterate `state.units` looking for `unit.type === "elon"`
2. Check adjacency: use `dr = -1..1, dc = -1..1` loop (skip `dr===0 && dc===0`), compare `elon.row === s.row + dr && elon.col === s.col + dc`
3. If adjacent, roll `Math.random() < 0.10`
4. On success: set `elon.name = "Elon Bones"`, `elon.movesLeft = 0`, `elon.movesMax = 0`, `state.gameOver = true`, call `addLog("...", "explosion")`
5. If gameOver, break out of the battery loop

## Patterns to match

- Follow the dust storm Elon death pattern (around line 1700-1706):
  ```js
  unit.name = "Elon Bones";
  unit.movesLeft = 0;
  unit.movesMax = 0;
  state.gameOver = true;
  addLog("Elon Musk was consumed by the Dust Storm! Only bones remain... GAME OVER!", "storm");
  ```
- Use `"explosion"` as the log type (consistent with existing battery explosion log)
- Use `s.col` and `s.row` for the battery position (the structure variable in the loop is `s`)
- Important: the structure is spliced BEFORE your check, so use the saved `s.col`/`s.row` values (they're still valid since `s` is a reference)

## Conventions
- Vanilla JS, var declarations, no ES6+ (match existing style)
- No new functions needed — inline in the existing loop
- Only check for Elon (type === "elon"), explicitly skip robots
- Log message should mention the battery explosion killing Elon and include GAME OVER

checklist.description: Add Elon adjacency check after battery explosion
steps: In processSubparBatteryExplosions(), after line 1746 (splice), add code to find Elon unit from state.units
Add dr/dc loop (-1 to 1) to check if Elon is on adjacent tile to exploded battery position
Verify adjacency check skips (0,0) center tile

checklist.description: Add 10% kill roll and Elon Bones death state
steps: If Elon is adjacent, roll Math.random() < 0.10
On success: set elon.name = 'Elon Bones', elon.movesLeft = 0, elon.movesMax = 0, state.gameOver = true
Add addLog call with type 'explosion' describing Elon killed by battery explosion
Break out of battery loop if gameOver is set

checklist.description: Battery still removed regardless of Elon check
steps: Verify the splice happens before the Elon check (battery is always removed)
Verify that if Elon is not adjacent or roll fails, loop continues normally

checklist.description: Robots are not affected by explosion
steps: Verify the adjacency check only looks for unit.type === 'elon'
Confirm no code path affects Rocktimus Robots

quality_checklist.description: Code matches existing codebase style (var declarations, no ES6+, consistent patterns)
steps: Check for let/const usage (should be var)
Verify pattern matches dust storm death code
Open browser to localhost:3000
Verify game canvas renders
Check browser console for JS errors

checks.description: Battery explosion Elon death code exists in processSubparBatteryExplosions
steps: Read game.js and find processSubparBatteryExplosions function
Verify Elon adjacency check exists after battery splice
Verify 10% roll and Elon Bones death state are implemented
Verify log entry with type 'explosion' is added
